defensetech.adverse-action-notice: DFARS 252.204-7012(c)(1)(ii) 72-hour wall-clock report to dibnet.dod.mil (SECOND Suite vertical with numeric wall-clock invariant; FIRST was EnergyTech CIP-008 1-hour) + DDTC voluntary disclosure (optional, ITAR breach) + BIS voluntary disclosure (optional, EAR breach) + FSO notification (always, on cleared facility)
defensetech.four-fifths-rule: OFCCP 4/5ths-rule on DIB workforce dimensions + VEVRAA protected-veteran preference + Section 503 7%-utilization goal + EO 12968 + 32 CFR 147 adjudicative-guidelines disparity tracking + NISPOM insider-threat-flag-rate per-1000 + SBA SB/SDB/VOSB/SDVOSB flow-down coverage % — 15 dimensions × 8 metric kinds
defensetech.protected-class-vault: FIRST Suite vault contract with 3 orthogonal typed policy axes (cui_categorization 9 tiers × export_control_status 4 tiers × foreign_person_access_restriction 5 tiers) — resolvePolicy() intersects all 3 axes at runtime. cross_binding_refs as REQUIRED schema fields publish sibling repos (audit-stream + evidence-bundle + incident-card). vs LegalTech 1-axis, EnergyTech 2-axis, every other vertical 1-axis equivalent
defensetech.regulator-referral: DoD CIO (DFARS 7012 cyber incident via dibnet.dod.mil) + Cyber AB (CMMC ecosystem) + DIBCAC (DCMA government-led L3 assessment) + DDTC (ITAR voluntary disclosure) + BIS (EAR voluntary disclosure + Entity List screening) + NIST (standards-incorporation-by-reference) + GSA (FAR + Schedule + FedRAMP joint authorization) + DCMA (CPSR + supplier surveillance) + DCSA (NISPOM + personnel + facility clearance) + NARA-ISOO (CUI Registry + CUI Notice 2020-04) — 10 federal authorities × 8 distinct enforcement modes (most diverse state-tracker in Suite). 22 event types on Incident Card — LARGEST Incident Card profile in Suite
